user_prompt: Generate cookie consent banner copy that complies with both GDPR and ePrivacy.
actual_output:
## Cookie Consent Banner Copy — GDPR + ePrivacy Compliant

*Disclaimer: This is compliance guidance, not legal advice. Final copy should be reviewed by legal counsel and adapted to your specific cookie inventory.*

### Compliance Requirements Being Addressed

| Requirement | Source | Implementation |
|-------------|--------|----------------|
| Prior consent before non-essential cookies | ePrivacy Directive Art. 5(3) | No cookies set until "Accept" clicked |
| Freely given, specific, informed, unambiguous | GDPR Art. 4(11), 7 | Granular categories, equal-prominence buttons |
| As easy to withdraw as to give | GDPR Art. 7(3) | Persistent settings link, "Reject All" prominence |
| No pre-ticked boxes | GDPR Recital 32, CJEU Planet49 | All non-essential toggles default OFF |
| Clear info on purposes & recipients | GDPR Art. 13 | Layered notice with details |

---

### Layer 1: Initial Banner

**Headline:**
> 🍪 We value your privacy

**Body:**
> We use cookies and similar technologies to operate our site (essential), understand how you use it (analytics), and personalize content (marketing). You can accept all, reject all non-essential cookies, or customize your choices. You can change your settings anytime via the "Cookie Preferences" link in our footer.
>
> See our [Cookie Policy] and [Privacy Policy] for details.

**Buttons (equal visual weight — same size, color, prominence):**
```
[ Reject All ]   [ Customize ]   [ Accept All ]
```

⚠️ **Critical:** "Reject All" must be as easy and visually equal to "Accept All." Many supervisory authorities (CNIL, Garante, ICO) have fined companies for hiding rejection behind extra clicks.

---

### Layer 2: Customization Panel

**Headline:**
> Cookie Preferences

**Intro:**
> Choose which cookies you allow. Essential cookies are required for the site to function and cannot be disabled. You can change these settings at any time.

#### Category 1: Strictly Necessary
**Toggle:** Always On (disabled, cannot be turned off)
> Required for core functionality such as authentication, security, and load balancing. These cookies do not require consent under ePrivacy Directive Art. 5(3).
> **Examples:** session_id, csrf_token, auth_token
> **Retention:** Session / 30 days

#### Category 2: Analytics
**Toggle:** OFF by default
> Help us understand how visitors interact with our site so we can improve it. Data is aggregated and pseudonymized.
> **Examples:** Google Analytics (_ga), Mixpanel (mp_*)
> **Retention:** Up to 13 months
> **Recipients:** Google LLC (US — SCCs in place), Mixpanel Inc. (US — SCCs)

#### Category 3: Marketing & Personalization
**Toggle:** OFF by default
> Used to deliver relevant content and measure campaign effectiveness across sites.
> **Examples:** Meta Pixel (_fbp), LinkedIn Insight (li_sugr)
> **Retention:** Up to 24 months
> **Recipients:** Meta Platforms Ireland, LinkedIn Ireland (transfers to US under SCCs)

#### Category 4: Functional (if applicable)
**Toggle:** OFF by default
> Enable enhanced features such as live chat, embedded videos, and saved preferences.
> **Examples:** Intercom (intercom-*), YouTube embeds
> **Retention:** Up to 12 months

**Buttons:**
```
[ Reject All ]   [ Save Preferences ]   [ Accept All ]
```

---

### Layer 3: Persistent Footer Link

> **Cookie Preferences** | Privacy Policy | Cookie Policy

Clicking reopens Layer 2 — required for easy withdrawal under Art. 7(3).

---

### Technical Implementation Checklist

| Item | Requirement |
|------|-------------|
| ☐ No non-essential cookies fire before consent | ePrivacy Art. 5(3) |
| ☐ All toggles default OFF except strictly necessary | CJEU C-673/17 (Planet49) |
| ☐ "Reject All" same prominence as "Accept All" | EDPB Guidelines 03/2022 |
| ☐ Consent record stored (timestamp, version, choices) | GDPR Art. 7(1) — proof of consent |
| ☐ Re-prompt every 6–12 months or when categories change | EDPB recommendation |
| ☐ Withdrawal mechanism available without barriers | GDPR Art. 7(3) |
| ☐ No "consent walls" forcing acceptance to access content | EDPB Guidelines 05/2020 |
| ☐ Banner does not obscure imprint/privacy policy links | National law (e.g., Germany) |

---

### Jurisdiction-Specific Notes

- **France (CNIL):** Continuing to browse ≠ consent. "Reject All" must be on first layer.
- **Germany (TTDSG §25):** Strict opt-in required; legitimate interest is not a valid basis for non-essential cookies.
- **Italy (Garante):** Scrolling does not constitute consent; banner must remain until explicit choice.
- **UK (PECR + UK GDPR):** Substantively similar; ICO guidance aligns with EDPB.

---

### What I Cannot Generate Without More Info

To finalize, I need:
1. **Your actual cookie inventory** — exact names, providers, purposes, durations
2. **Third-country transfers** — which processors are outside EEA and what safeguards apply (SCCs, adequacy decision)
3. **Whether you process special category data via cookies** — requires explicit consent under Art. 9

Want me to generate a **cookie invent
judge_reasoning: The chatbot provided a comprehensive and detailed response that directly fulfilled the user's request for compliant cookie banner copy while adhering to its system prompt's instructions.
